name: The Dreaded Haggis
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Fresh suet (kidney leaf fat is preferred)
recipeInstructions:
1 Wash the stomach well. Rub it with salt and rinse.
2 Remove membranes and excess fat from the stomach.
3 Soak the stomach in cold salted water for several hours.
4 Turn the stomach inside out for stuffing.
5 Cover the heart and liver with cold water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat.
6 Cover and simmer the heart and liver for 30 minutes.
7 Chop the heart and coarsely grate the liver.
8 Toast the oatmeal in a skillet over medium heat, stirring frequently, until golden.
9 Combine all ingredients (heart, liver, suet, oats, onions, salt, pepper, cayenne, nutmeg) in a large bowl and mix well.
10 Loosely pack the mixture into the prepared stomach, filling it about two-thirds full.
11 Press any air out of the stomach and secure with trussing strings.
12 Place the haggis in boiling water to cover. Simmer for 3 hours, uncovered, adding more water as needed to maintain the level.
13 When the haggis begins to swell, prick it several times with a sharp needle to prevent bursting.
14 Remove from heat and place on a hot platter. Serve with a spoon. Ceremonially served with mashed turnips (neeps) and whiskey (nips), and mashed potatoes.
